landing.heroWedge: AI writes the code. You learn why it works.
landing.heroSubtitle: Real code in your browser. XP for every lesson. A credential that lives in your wallet.
landing.step1Desc: Rust, Anchor and TypeScript challenges in your browser, graded by hidden tests. No setup, no videos to sit through.
landing.step2Desc: Lessons mint soulbound XP straight to your wallet. Levels, streaks and daily quests keep score on a live leaderboard.
landing.step3Desc: Finish a course and mint a soulbound credential NFT. Anyone can verify it on-chain. Superteam Earn bounties are the next step.
landing.step3Hint: click the card to flip it
landing.featureCommunityDesc: Ask questions, share knowledge, and earn XP from helping others. Peer learning with voting and accepted answers.
landing.featureStreaksDesc: Keep your streak alive with daily completions. Bonus XP for finishing daily quests.
landing.featureOssDesc: Built by Superteam Brasil, open to everyone. Fork it, extend it, make it yours.
landing.gamificationCopy: Earn XP for every lesson. Unlock {count} achievements. Compete on the leaderboard.
landing.ctaAuthMethods: Sign in with your wallet, Google, or GitHub

console.title: Admin Console
console.subtitle: Publish course content, deploy courses on chain, moderate the community.

nav.pendingFlags: {count, plural, one {# pending flag} other {# pending flags}}

intro.lede: Getting a course to learners takes two separate steps, in order. This screen holds both: publish on top, deploy below.
intro.step1Title: 1. Publish — does the app have this course?
intro.step1Body: Course content lives in the courses-academy repo. The app ships a compiled copy of it, pinned to one commit in content.lock. Publishing is a human pull request that bumps that pin and rebuilds the bundle; once it merges, Vercel redeploys and the content is in the app. This screen writes nothing — it shows the drift and hands you a prefilled PR.
intro.step2Title: 2. Deploy — is the course on chain, and can learners see it?
intro.step2Body: Content sitting in the bundle stays invisible until the course has an on-chain account and is active. Deploying creates or updates that account. Only on-chain fields matter here: XP per lesson, creator reward, minimum completions, lesson count, and the immutable set (creator, difficulty, track, track level, prerequisite).
intro.contentOnlyNote: Editing lesson content — prose, code, quizzes, the title, tags — is not on chain. Those edits go live on publish alone, and correctly show nothing to deploy below. A course still reading "Synced" after you rewrote a lesson is not a bug.

coursesScreen.step1Eyebrow: Step 1 — Publish: get the content into the app
coursesScreen.step2Eyebrow: Step 2 — Deploy: put the course on chain

legend.title: What the states mean
legend.description: Every course row carries an on-chain state, and — once deployed — a content-drift state. They are independent.
legend.not_deployed: Never deployed. The course is in the bundle but has no on-chain account, so learners cannot see it. Deploy creates it.
legend.synced: On chain, and its on-chain fields match the bundle. Nothing to do.
legend.out_of_sync: On chain, but an on-chain field (XP per lesson, creator reward, minimum completions, lesson count) differs from the bundle. Deploy again to update it.
legend.draft: Marked as a draft in the content repo. Not deployable until it is no longer a draft.
legend.missing_fields: The bundle is missing on-chain fields this course needs before it can be deployed. The row lists which ones.
legend.drifted: The content repo has moved ahead of the pinned commit. The fix is a publish PR (step 1), not a deploy.
legend.blocked: Newer content exists upstream, but its CI is red. Do not publish it until CI is green.
legend.unknown: GitHub could not be reached, so drift could not be checked. The on-chain state is still accurate.
legend.immutableTitle: Immutable mismatch (no badge — the row shows a warning instead)
legend.immutableBody: Creator, difficulty, track, track level and prerequisite are written once, at first deploy, and can never be changed. If one of them differs from the bundle, the Deploy button disappears — an update cannot fix it. The only remedies are to restore the value in the content repo, or deactivate this course and deploy a new one.

publishPin.title: Content pin
publishPin.description: The committed content bundle is pinned to a courses-academy commit in content.lock. Publishing new content is a one-line human PR that bumps the pin and rebuilds the bundle — no server-side write happens here.
publishPin.unavailable: Drift unavailable — GitHub could not be reached. Try again shortly.
publishPin.upToDate: Up to date — the pin matches courses-academy HEAD.
publishPin.behind: {count, plural, one {# commit behind HEAD} other {# commits behind HEAD}}

prepare.title: Prepare publish PR
prepare.redHeadWarning: HEAD's CI is not green. Do not bump the pin to unverified content — wait for CI to pass.
prepare.step1: 1. Apply this one-line change to content.lock
prepare.step2Hint: Run this after editing {path}, then commit both the lock and the regenerated bundle.
prepare.step3: 3. Push a branch and open the PR

deployScreen.noCourses: No courses in the content bundle.
deployScreen.noAchievements: No achievements in the content bundle.
deployScreen.syncAllConfirm: {count, plural, one {Sync # course} other {Sync # courses}} — {changes, plural, one {# field change} other {# field changes}}. Continue?
deployScreen.deactivateConfirm: Deactivate this course? Students cannot enroll until reactivated.

contentDrift.driftedHint: Newer course content exists upstream in courses-academy. The fix is a publish PR that bumps content.lock and commits the rebuilt bundle — not a redeploy.
contentDrift.blockedHint: Upstream content changed but its CI is red — it cannot be synced yet.
contentDrift.unknownHint: Could not reach GitHub to check for content drift.

preview.title: Review deploy — {title}
preview.firstDeploy: This course is not on chain yet — deploying will create its on-chain account.
preview.contentStale: For information: this course's on-chain content commitment (content_tx_id) is behind the committed bundle. This deploy will NOT change it — it only writes the course fields listed above. The commitment is updated by the content-commit step on the Content Drift screen.
preview.blocked: Deploy is blocked until the immutable mismatch is resolved.

immutableWarning.heading: Immutable field mismatch — cannot auto-fix
immutableWarning.creatorEmphasis: Creator mismatch: the on-chain reward recipient does not match the instructor wallet. Course.creator is set once at deploy and can never be changed — every future creator reward would pay the wrong wallet. Recreating the course is the only fix.
immutableWarning.remediation: "{title}" has immutable on-chain fields that differ from the content bundle; update_course cannot apply them. Options: 1. Fix the value in the courses-academy repo and publish a new bundle PR, or 2. Deactivate this course and deploy a new version.

resync.heading: Data Resync
resync.description: Rebuild a user's Supabase data from on-chain state: XP balance (Token-2022 ATA), enrollments & lesson progress (Enrollment PDAs + bitmap), achievements (AchievementReceipt PDAs) & certificates (Enrollment credential_asset). Use after webhook failures or for migration.

flags.description: Community posts reported by users. Resolve once you've actioned the content, or dismiss if the report isn't valid.
flags.errorFetch: Failed to update the flag. Please try again.
